# SPEC-0.0.1.qmd

Read DOC-GUIDE-v001.qmd for documentation navigation and version numbering.  
Refer to the latest PRD for the full system vision. This spec defines the **initial MVP** only—a subset of the full system.

---

## MVP Scope Summary

### In scope (MVP)
- **Question storage**: Database is the source of truth for listing, ownership, and status; question content is stored on the filesystem (local) or in Supabase Storage. When using the filesystem, the app can mirror metadata in an optional `db_meta.yaml` per version and sync FS with DB (import FS-only, DB wins on conflict). See SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC-0.0.3.qmd.
- **Question creation**: Users create and upload new questions or modifications via the app. Any user can propose a new version of any question; the version is **pending** until the question owner approves it. Only approved versions are used in tests.
- **Authentication**: Email/password with full email confirmation workflow via Supabase (learners and creators). Optionally Google and/or GitHub OAuth via Supabase; each auth option is implemented and documented separately so the team can ship email/password first and add OAuth later if desired.
- **Student experience**: Take tests, answer questions (including coding in a sandboxed environment), submit, and see feedback.
- **Question management**: Create and upload new questions; propose or upload new versions (owner's versions are approved automatically; others are pending until owner approves); view metadata (e.g. created/modified).
- **Progress and integrity**: Store student progress (attempts, timestamps, question id/version, timing); basic anti-cheat (keystroke timing, copy/paste controls); comprehensive logging.

### Out of scope (MVP)
- (Question metadata and listing are database-backed; content lives in FS or Supabase Storage; FS can mirror with sync. See SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC.)
- Full tutorial authoring or tutorial flows.
- Advanced analytics, reporting, or social features.
- None; all listed auth options (email/password, Google OAuth, GitHub OAuth) are in scope for v0.0.1, but each is optional and can be implemented independently.

---

## Questions: Storage and database

### Rationale
Question **content** is stored on the **filesystem** (local dev) or in **Supabase Storage** (e.g. Vercel). The **database** (`question_versions` table) is the source of truth for listing, ownership, status, and which version is "latest." This allows:
- Users to develop and version questions on their own machine (e.g. with Git) when using FS.
- The app to have a single source of truth (DB) for who owns what and what is approved.
- An optional FS mirror with sync: FS-only versions can be imported into the DB; when both exist and differ, DB wins. See **SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC-0.0.3.qmd** for dual-write, `db_meta.yaml`, and sync behaviour.

### Layout and metadata
- **One folder per question version.**  
  See **docs/QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd** for the canonical folder layout, file names, metadata schema, and **all edge cases** (e.g. prompt file when both `prompt.md` and `prompt.txt` exist, id/type/version validation, type-specific required files, multiple-choice correctness). Implementations MUST follow that document. An optional **db_meta.yaml** in each version folder stores app metadata (owner_id, status, proposed_by) for sync; question content stays in meta.yaml and type-specific files only.
- **Required metadata** (per question, in meta.yaml and DB):
  - **Unique id** (e.g. UUID or slug) — stable across versions.
  - **Question type** — e.g. multiple choice, short answer, long answer, R coding, Bash, Excel formula, HTML, CSS (subset as needed for MVP).
  - **Version** — so modifications can be tracked and linked to student attempts.
- **Optional metadata**: title, domain, difficulty, rubric reference, etc., as defined in the question format doc.

### Question workflow
- User creates/edits the question folder and files locally (no need to be connected to the system).
- User **uploads** via the app:
  - **New question**: upload a folder (or archive) that conforms to the question format; system assigns or validates id and stores under the configured questions root. Version is **approved** (owner is the uploader).
  - **Modification to existing question**: upload updated files for an existing id; system stores a new version. If the uploader is the question owner, the version is **approved**; otherwise it is **pending** until the owner approves it. Only approved versions are used in tests.
- System logs **when** each question was created and when it was last modified (and by whom, if auth provides it).

---

## Authentication

Authentication in v0.0.1 is **modular**: the tech team may implement one or more of the following. Each option is specified and documented so it can be implemented **individually**; if the team cannot implement all in v0.0.1, they can pick and choose. Most likely, **email/password** is implemented first, then OAuth providers as needed.

### Email/password (primary; implement first)
- **Provider**: Supabase Auth email/password.
- **Email confirmation**: Full workflow — user signs up → system sends confirmation email → user clicks link (or enters code) → account is confirmed → user can sign in. Confirmation is required before the user can access protected flows.
- **Backend**: Supabase Auth (session management, user records). No third-party OAuth app setup required.
- **Behavior**: Users sign up, confirm email, then sign in; session is used for all subsequent requests; student progress and logs are tied to the authenticated user.

### Google OAuth (optional)
- **Provider**: Google OAuth via Supabase Auth. Can be added alongside or after email/password.
- **Backend**: Same Supabase Auth; enable Google in Supabase Dashboard (or local config) and implement the Google sign-in flow in the app. Documented separately in SETUP so it can be implemented on its own.

### GitHub OAuth (optional)
- **Provider**: GitHub OAuth via Supabase Auth. Can be added alongside or after email/password.
- **Backend**: Same Supabase Auth; enable GitHub in Supabase Dashboard (or local config) and implement the GitHub sign-in flow in the app. Documented separately in SETUP so it can be implemented on its own.

### Roles and behavior (all providers)
- **Roles**: All authenticated users can take tests, create questions, and propose new versions. There is no separate "author" role; question **ownership** is per question (the user who created the first approved version). Only the owner can approve pending versions proposed by others.
- **Unified behavior**: Regardless of which provider a user signed in with, the app treats them as the same authenticated user; progress and logs are tied to the Supabase user id.

---

## Frontend — Question creation and management

- **Create/upload new questions**: Any authenticated user can create questions (form or file upload). Server validates per QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd. If validation fails, upload is **rejected** with clear errors; if it passes, the question is stored and the version is **approved** (uploader is owner).
- **Propose new versions**: Any user can propose a new version of any question (form or upload). If the user is the question owner, the version is **approved**; otherwise it is **pending** until the owner approves it. Owner sees "Pending your approval" and can approve or ignore.
- **Upload modifications**: UI to select an existing question (by id) and upload updated files; server validates per format. Non-owner uploads create a **pending** version; owner uploads create an **approved** version.
- **Metadata display**: List or detail view of questions showing at least: question id, type, version, status (approved/pending), when created and when last modified.
- **Logging**: All upload and metadata-view actions are logged (who, when, which question id/version).

---

## Frontend — Students (Learners)

- **Question set selection**: List of available question sets (from DB and/or filesystem; see SPEC-QUESTION-SETS-0.0.2) (tests can be defined in config or filesystem; which tests are “active” is MVP-defined).
- **Taking a set as test**: 
  - Student selects a question set and chooses "Take as test"; the app starts a session and presents questions (order can be fixed or randomized per MVP choice).
  - For each question: prompt and any assets are shown; student answers (text, choice, or code as appropriate).
- **Coding questions**: For R, Bash, or other code-based types, the student is presented with a **sandboxed coding and execution environment** (e.g. in-browser or backed by a secure runner). Student writes code, runs it, and submits the final answer; execution is constrained per “Sandbox isolation” below.
- **Submission**: Student submits the test; answers are evaluated (per question type) and feedback is shown.
- **Logging**: All student actions are logged (session start/end, question view, answer edit, run code, submit) for auditing and anti-cheat.

---

## Sandboxed Execution (Coding Questions)

- **Purpose**: Run user-submitted code (e.g. R, Bash) safely for grading.
- **Minimum viable isolation** (aligned with PRD):
  - No access to host filesystem, network, or environment secrets.
  - CPU and memory limits and execution timeouts.
- **MVP implementation**: To be chosen (e.g. container-based, serverless runner, or managed service); the interface used by the app is “run this code with these inputs” and “return stdout/stderr and exit code.”
- **Logging**: Every execution (question id, version, user, code hash or length, inputs, result) is logged for security and debugging.

---

## Student Progress and Storage

Student progress is stored in **database and/or filesystem** (MVP can choose one or both). The following must be recorded:

- **Per attempt (per session)**:
  - User id, session/set id, question id, **question version**.
  - Date/time when the question was presented and when the answer was submitted.
  - Time between presentation and submission (and optionally time per question).
- **Anti-cheat and integrity**:
  - **Keystroke timing**: Average (or distribution of) time between keystrokes where applicable (e.g. text/code answers) to help detect pasted content or automated input.
  - **Copy/paste controls**: Ability to **discourage or prevent** copy/paste of question text or answers (e.g. disable paste in code/text areas, or log paste events). Exact UX (prevent vs warn vs log-only) is an MVP decision.
- **Stored data**: At minimum, question id, version, timestamps, answer (or reference to it), evaluation result (score/feedback), and any keystroke/paste metrics agreed for MVP.

---

## Logging

- **Principle**: Log everything that is needed for security, auditing, and debugging.
- **Scope** (non-exhaustive):
  - **Auth**: sign-in, sign-out, failures.
  - **Questions**: uploads (new question / modification), metadata views, approval of pending versions (by owner).
  - **Students**: test start/stop, question view, answer change, code run, submit; optional: keystroke/paste events if collected.
  - **System**: sandbox runs (inputs/outputs hashed or truncated if large), evaluator errors, upload validation failures.
- **Format and retention**: Logs should be structured (e.g. JSON) and stored in a dedicated store or filesystem; retention period is an MVP/deployment decision.

---

## Technical Stack (MVP)

- **Frontend**: Next.js.
- **Auth & DB**: Supabase (email/password with email confirmation; optionally Google and/or GitHub OAuth; user tables, question_versions, question_sets, test_sessions, answers). Each auth method is configured and implemented independently per SETUP.
- **Hosting**: Vercel (or equivalent) for Next.js and serverless/API routes.
- **Questions**: Database is source of truth for listing and ownership; content in filesystem (configured root) or Supabase Storage. Format and metadata as in QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd; optional db_meta.yaml and sync per SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC-0.0.3.qmd.
- **Execution**: Sandbox implementation TBD (containers, serverless, or managed service).

---

## Question Types in MVP

Support a **subset** of the PRD question types to keep MVP shippable. Recommended minimum:

1. **Multiple choice** — deterministic.
2. **Short answer** — AI or rule-based grading; deterministic scoring.
3. **At least one code type** — e.g. **R** or **Bash** — using the sandbox; output comparison for grading.

Additional types (long answer, Excel, HTML, CSS) can be added incrementally; each requires an evaluator module and, for code/formula, sandbox or equivalent.

---

## Open Items / TODOs

- [ ] Finalize question folder layout and metadata schema in QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd.
- [ ] Finalize API contracts for upload, question-set listing, and submission (see SPEC-QUESTION-SETS and implemented routes).
- [ ] Choose sandbox implementation and document security constraints.
- [ ] Define evaluator output schema (score, pass/fail, feedback) for each supported type.
- [ ] Student progress: stored in DB (test_sessions, answers); see migrations.
- [ ] Question sets ("tests") are composed via question_sets and question_set_items (DB) and/or set.yaml (filesystem); see SPEC-QUESTION-SETS-0.0.2.
- [ ] Specify log format, storage, and retention.

---

## Related Documents

- **DOC-GUIDE-v001.qmd** — Documentation and versioning.
- **PRD-0.0.1-v003.qmd** — Full product vision and requirements.
- **QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1.qmd** — Question folder layout, metadata schema, and optional db_meta.yaml.
- **SPEC-QUESTION-SETS-0.0.2.qmd** — Question sets (DB + file-based), take as test.
- **SPEC-DB-FS-QUESTION-SYNC-0.0.3.qmd** — DB–FS question sync, dual-write, and backup.
